title: Landfall
pov_character: Kirin Saleh (primary); Ade (cold open); Berin Kosta (interlude)
summary: Kirin walks five days northeast from the transit gate, reading clay discs at junction posts, sheltering through rain, reading Adaeze's log through 2124 at the end of each day. She arrives at the second way-station — a repurposed agricultural processing building now serving as the interior relay network's hub — and meets Ade, the relay manager. Ade tells her Nnedi Eze died two months ago. Kirin opens Adaeze's sealed envelope: the third instruction was never 'find Nnedi' — it is 'take the key to the plateau and let what happens happen.' At the communal kitchen she meets Lupe, a former Compact Keeper who has been in the settlement seventeen years, who explains the cedar grove reparation process and tells her to speak to Saren. Saren knew Adaeze for twenty-two years and describes her as respected and inconvenient — not warm, useful, and occasionally irritating. Saren describes Nnedi Eze: effective, driven, divisive, difficult to live with, and the builder of something that will last. Whether Nnedi gave her Rootstock key to anyone is unknown; Saren directs Kirin to ask Rua, who was with Nnedi at the end. Berin Kosta, fourteen months inside the Outside at the archive node in sector twelve, receives the relay about a new bearer and decides to come in a week when his cataloguing work allows. Kirin leaves the settlement the next morning with the plateau route from Pit and Ade's note about Rua.
locations: The second way-station (former agricultural processing facility, interior relay hub)
The open ground northeast of the transit gate (five-day walking route)
themes: Instructions written for circumstances that change
The difference between what a person built and what a person was
Relay networks as infrastructure of trust
Two kinds of maintenance: structural and sentimental
continuity_notes: Nnedi Eze died January 2126, two months before Kirin arrived. Whether she passed on her Rootstock key is unknown — ask Rua (plateau council infrastructure lead, was with Nnedi at the end). Berin Kosta is alive and working at the archive node in sector twelve; he received the relay about Kirin and intends to come in one week. Ade is the relay manager at the second way-station; she is on the management committee of the settlement's resource governance. Kirin leaves with the plateau route (from Pit) — four days northeast, ridge approach then western descent into the basin. Her note to Berin is: find me at Rua's if you come before I leave. The sealed envelope has been opened and its contents received. The third instruction's shape has changed: not a delivery but a presence — bring the key to the plateau and let what happens happen. Kirin does not know what 'what happens' is.
